title: Train Speed Logic
prompt: A train travels from City A to City B at 60 km/h and returns at 40 km/h. What is the average speed for the entire journey? Show your reasoning step by step.

title: Logical Deduction
prompt: All doctors are professionals. Some professionals are wealthy. Can we conclude that some doctors are wealthy? Explain your reasoning.
explanation: No. The wealthy professionals could be a different subset than doctors. The premises don't guarantee overlap between doctors and wealthy people.

title: Probability Reasoning
prompt: A bag contains 4 red balls and 6 blue balls. If you draw 2 balls without replacement, what is the probability that both are red? Show your work.
explanation: P(first red) = 4/10. P(second red | first red) = 3/9. P(both) = 4/10 * 3/9 = 12/90 = 2/15

title: Causal Reasoning
prompt: A company's sales increased by 20% after they started a new ad campaign AND hired a new sales director at the same time. A manager concludes the ad campaign caused the sales increase. Is this reasoning valid? Why or why not?
explanation: No. This is a post hoc / confounding variable fallacy. Both changes happened simultaneously, so we cannot attribute causation to the ad campaign alone without controlling for the new director's impact.

title: Sequence Pattern
prompt: What is the next number in the sequence: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ? Explain the pattern you found.

title: Analogy Reasoning
prompt: Doctor is to Hospital as Teacher is to ___? Now apply the same relationship: Pilot is to ___? Explain the relationship pattern.
explanation: Doctor works in a Hospital. Teacher works in a School. Pilot works in/on an Airplane. The relationship is professional to their primary workplace/vehicle.
